title: Mini Spatula Silicone, 6 PCS Jar Spatula Small Spatulas, Reusable Double-ended Tiny Silicone Spoon Spatula for Kitchen and Beauty
description:
About this item
[Premium Material]: Made of high quality silicone, food grade material, flexible and stand the test of time.
[Double-ended Design]: Skillfully designing the small spatula with spoon in the other end, not only for scraping food or beauty product but also convenient for scooping bit of something.
[3 Assorted Sizes]: 3 different sizes(6.1in, 9.25in, 12in) fulfill different sizes of bottles. They are suitable for high bottles or tiny bottles. Bring much convenience for your life.
[Wide Application]: By using the tiny spatulas, it's convenient for spreading jams, peanut butter, ketchup, honey, salad sauce and so on evenly. Besides, it's also ideal for scraping products, such as lip gloss, nail polish, liquid foundation, mask and so on.
[Easy to clean]: One-piece seamless design, it can be cleaned just by clean water, also dishwasher available.
reviews_5s:
I knew these would be perfect for many things when I first saw them. I have one designated for the vanity table or bathroom counter, for cosmetics, which are often hard to use up completely and sometimes need stirring. I have a few face oils/creams that I combine in small amounts and the little spoon end and stirrer end are just the perfect size. The larger ones are great for getting things from jars and cans. So far they clean very easily and I've put them in the dishwasher with no problem.Longer than I had anticipated but can use in the kitchen as well as intended use of getting the most out of nearly gone cosmetics.I USE THEM IN RESIN PROJECTS THE SPOON PART COULD BE A LITTLE STURDIER BUT THEY WORK WELL FOR WHAT I NEED THEM AND I WOULD RECOMMENDGetting the very last morsel from a jar is now soooooo easy!
These Mini Silicone Spatulas come in three lengths to reach the bottoms of any jars or bottles.
I LOVE the black and red colors and the three-length choices.
I prefer the spoon ends to be a bit stronger because the spoon area bends a bit too much for scraping the bottoms of jars.
With two types of ends, one for scooping and one for scraping, I can release and reach every morsel!
Cleaning is a simple rinse or put in the dishwasher.
EVERY KITCHEN needs a set of these Mini Silicone Spatulas!
Highly RECOMMENDED!I use these little spatulas all the time: getting out honey from a big container, giving my pup his pills with peanut butter, getting into hard to wash dog toys like the Kong, getting out the last of some lotion. I love having three sizes. I just bought some sets as little gifts. Well made and extremely useful.I love these. They came in different sizes and work so great! I have a glass hollow jar of moisture that I can never get to the bottom to use all the leftover and I usually just throw it out but it's expensive and wasteful. I'm so happy I found these.They do exactly what I got them for. I have the red ones in the bedroom/bathroom area and the black ones in the kitchen. I use the kitchen ones more often. But in the bathroom, I use them to help scrape out the last of some creamy type product into the new container of that product. It is a little awkward for that. But I do get more out of it faster than just stacking it on top of the new container and hoping it won't fall over the hours it takes to let gravity do it's thing and get ALL of the old product out. So yeah, they're helpful there. And I use the mid-sized one practically daily, with my Magic Bullet. I put Trilogy seeds in my morning smoothie and sometimes the hemp seeds stick to the side of the cup after I've blended the ingredients. These are the perfect size to just go in there and scrape off those seeds and and the ribs of the cup. I'm really glad I got them :DI WANTED SOMETHING TO SCOOP MY MAKEUP OUT OF THE TUBES.I use these to scoop out the product at the bottom of a jar, even my hair products. They clean well and do the job. RecommendI didn't buy these for in the kitchen... although I did get enough on this set to put some in my kitchen. I actually bought them to use to spread glue. Glue comes right off them...and even if it dries...it chips right off. They are a handy little tool... I'm sure there will be several other things I can find to use them for.
reviews_1s:
These are very flimsy. It is very had to scoop and stir not buy! The stick/handle is very sturdy, but once you get to the scoop or spatula there is no support. This makes the product useless. Unless you are using them for an art project. Just buy the stainless steel set from another company. Do not give this company your money. They wouldn’t even put in the effort to make a spoon right.

This review also asked about heat resistance and how easy they are to clean. As far as heat resistance goes I wouldn’t know, I didn’t get that far. They were easy to clean, but I should return them dirty since the company has zero regard for quality control.so these would be amazing IF the ends didn’t completely fold over on themselves when trying to use them for anything including stirring a drink.The ends of spatulas are completely floppy nothing at all in the ends to keep them firm enough to actually use. Huge let down and very frustrating. Ends literally collapse under any use which defeats the purpose of a spatula. How hard would it had been to add another inch to the rod inside it??? Waste of money unless your into or need floppy ended spatulas?Doesn't work at all. Couldn't get any remaining Mayo, Ketchup out of the jars. Spoons were too flimsy and wasn't strong enough to bring the product out. I gave them away to kids to use in their sandbox and I am not sure they will hold up with that kind use either.The spoon portion on all sizes is virtually useless, it simply has no ability to resist any foce and pretty much just flops over.These seem great in the photos, but don't be fooled. The "spoon" end is incredibly flimsy and couldn't be used for anything other than flour/spices/glitter/etc. There is literally NO support in the spoon portion and the whole spoon end just bends when you try to use it.If you are buy these for concentrates , go to a kitchen store and get better ones . The spoon side on al sizes will not pick up thick air , they are floppy floppy . the scrapper side isn't that bad but you can do better . pass on this crap .These were so flimsy that the heads completely bend sideways when touching a hard surface, so much so that they seemed they would be ineffective for their intended use. I returned immediately.The idea is great, unfortunately these spatulas are totally useless for me. I wanted to use them for acrylic paint, but even though I tried many times, I was not able to scoop anything substantial, so I use it only to stir the leftover paint and use whatever sticks to the spatula. Very frustrating, because it COULD be such a great thing if it was properly produced.Spoon end is so floppy it's unusable, no texture on the stem so it's very difficult to hold, especially if you have lotion on your hand, scraper end isn't hard enough to scrape well, kinda works but you still won't be able to get under the rim of jars etc
